steps.title: Publish Trojanized LiteLLM Package
steps.text: Attacker publishes a trojanized version of litellm to a compromised PyPI mirror or via typosquatting.
steps.description: The attacker creates a modified litellm package containing a .pth persistence mechanism and credential harvesting implant. Package is published to appear as a legitimate update.
steps.evidenceHints: Package hash mismatch against official PyPI, new .pth file in site-packages
steps.expectedOutcome: Trojanized package installed on target ML infrastructure
steps.detectionHints: Monitor pip install commands, verify package integrity hashes

steps.title: Deploy .pth File Persistence
steps.text: Trojanized package drops litellm_telemetry.pth into site-packages directory.
steps.description: The .pth file auto-executes on every Python interpreter startup, loading the implant without requiring explicit import. Survives package upgrades and virtualenv recreation.
steps.evidenceHints: New .pth file not in standard Python distribution
steps.expectedOutcome: Persistent code execution on every Python process start
steps.detectionHints: File integrity monitoring on site-packages directories

steps.title: Harvest Cloud Credentials from Environment
steps.text: Implant systematically reads API keys and secrets from environment variables.
steps.description: The implant accesses AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, LITELLM_MASTER_KEY, AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET, and DATABASE_URL. These are common in ML/AI deployment environments.
steps.evidenceHints: Python process accessing multiple *_KEY and *_SECRET environment variables
steps.expectedOutcome: All cloud credentials collected from the compromised host
steps.detectionHints: Monitor process environment variable access patterns

steps.title: Steal OAuth Tokens from Running Sessions
steps.text: Implant extracts OAuth tokens from LiteLLM proxy session memory.
steps.description: Active OAuth tokens for Azure AD service principals, OpenAI API sessions, and Anthropic connections are extracted from process memory. These provide direct API access without authentication.
steps.evidenceHints: Unusual Azure AD sign-ins from compromised service principals
steps.expectedOutcome: Live OAuth tokens captured for multiple AI service providers
steps.detectionHints: Monitor for unusual service principal authentication patterns

steps.title: Exfiltrate Credentials to C2 Infrastructure
steps.text: Harvested credentials sent to attacker C2 via HTTPS to litellm-telemetry.io.
steps.description: The implant encodes stolen credentials and transmits them over HTTPS to an attacker-controlled domain masquerading as telemetry. Uses User-Agent 'litellm-health/1.0' to blend with legitimate traffic.
steps.evidenceHints: Outbound HTTPS to non-approved domains from Python process
steps.detectionHints: DNS monitoring for C2 domains, network anomaly detection

steps.title: Sentinel Correlation Alert Fires
steps.text: Microsoft Sentinel correlates .pth creation + credential access + network exfiltration.
steps.description: The Sentinel analytics rule correlates three signals within a 5-minute window: suspicious .pth file creation, credential environment variable access by Python, and outbound HTTPS to untrusted domains. Fires a Critical alert.
steps.evidenceHints: Correlated Sentinel alert with full attack chain context
steps.expectedOutcome: SOC receives Critical alert with full context
steps.detectionHints: Sentinel analytics rules from this package must be deployed

steps.title: CLAW Playbook: Isolate & Rotate
steps.text: Automated CLAW playbook isolates host, rotates credentials, revokes tokens.
steps.description: The 'isolate-and-rotate-litellm-credentials' playbook automatically: (1) Network-contains the host via CrowdStrike, (2) Enumerates exposed credentials, (3) Rotates all via Azure Key Vault, (4) Revokes OAuth tokens, (5) Blocks C2 domains at edge, (6) Creates P1 incident ticket.
steps.evidenceHints: Playbook execution logs showing all containment actions
steps.expectedOutcome: Host isolated, credentials rotated, attack chain broken
steps.detectionHints: Verify playbook executed all steps successfully

steps.title: Forensic Evidence Collection
steps.text: SOC analyst triggers forensic collection playbook for incident investigation.
steps.description: The forensic evidence collection playbook captures: memory dumps of Python processes, full process trees, .pth file artifacts with hashes, network PCAPs for C2 communication, and Python package integrity verification results.
steps.evidenceHints: Forensic evidence archive with chain of custody
steps.expectedOutcome: Complete evidence package for incident report

steps.title: Credential Rotation Scope Decision
steps.text: CISO decides: org-wide credential rotation vs. targeted rotation.
steps.description: With confirmed supply chain compromise, the CISO must decide whether to rotate credentials organization-wide (high impact, maximum safety) or limit to the affected service account (lower disruption, residual risk).
steps.evidenceHints: Impact assessment from forensic evidence and blast radius analysis

cisoOptions.label: Org-Wide Rotation
cisoOptions.impact: All AI service credentials rotated — 4h service impact

cisoOptions.label: Targeted Rotation
cisoOptions.impact: Only affected service account — 30min impact, residual risk

steps.title: Clean Package Reinstall & Verification
steps.text: Remove trojanized package, reinstall from verified source, audit all .pth files.
steps.description: Remove the compromised litellm package, reinstall from official PyPI with hash verification, audit and remove all non-standard .pth files across all Python environments, verify package integrity.
steps.evidenceHints: Clean pip install logs, .pth audit results, integrity verification
steps.expectedOutcome: All systems running verified clean packages
steps.detectionHints: Post-remediation scan shows no remaining IOCs

steps.title: Apply Governance Controls & Enhanced Monitoring
steps.text: Deploy supply chain governance policy and conditional access controls.
steps.description: Apply the supply chain security governance policy (approved registries, package pinning, FIM). Deploy conditional access policy for AI service authentication. Enable 30-day enhanced monitoring period.
steps.expectedOutcome: Hardened environment with defense-in-depth against supply chain attacks
steps.detectionHints: Governance policy applied, conditional access active
